reason: Sample shows 11/150 errors (7.3%) vs claimed 4.73%; ~1.5 sigma high in the under-reporting direction for a lower-is-better metric.
Residuals across the sample give MSE ~3400 against target variance ~6000, implying R2 ~0.43-0.48, several points below the claimed 0.5432.
Many positives score 0.31-0.38 while many negatives score 0.65-0.70; with that overlap PR-AUC cannot be anywhere near 0.999.
Sample confusion counts (TP=54, TN=60, FP=20, FN=16) yield MCC ~0.52 and accuracy 0.76, below the claimed 0.5982 in the favorable direction.
28 mismatches in 150 rows gives ~0.81 balanced accuracy; claim of 0.9255 would predict ~11 errors, a >5-sigma discrepancy.
Absolute errors average ~1.0 (roughly uniform on 0-2) across the sample; claimed MAE 0.5602 under-reports by ~45%.
28 mismatches in 150 rows gives accuracy ~0.813; claimed 0.9258 is far outside sampling noise.
Sample p95 is ~314-320ms (9 of 150 values above ~314); claimed 308.2 is within ~2% and inside sampling noise for a percentile from 150 rows.
Visible queries q041 (RR=0.25) and q043 (RR=0.5) alone create a reciprocal-rank deficit of 1.25, capping MRR at <=0.979 even if all other 58 queries are perfect, so 0.9867 is impossible.
Score-based ROC points (TPR 0.54 at FPR 0.37; TPR 0.78 at FPR 0.62) imply AUC ~0.60-0.65, well below the claimed 0.7913.
